title: What would you do about this
display_title: What would you do about this
wikitext:
[]: What would you do about this?             Quote Post by Radi » Sat Jul 31, 2010 11:44 pm I just started Evangelion yesterday, and I watched through both movies (1.0, and 2.2. Didn't get a download for 1.1, but I might go ahead, and get it later), and surprise, surprise, I fell in love with it. I wanted to know, since I've already watched both of the movies they've released, if it's wise to start the series now, or...Inevitably wait..Two more years, till' it's all over with, and finish it off from there. You may be saying, "It's four years from now, so just finish it!" Well, I guess that is true, but I'm just trying to clarify. I've really loved the movies, so I guess I'm a bit nervous about spoiling the secrets before seeing the movies. Many-a-thanks to those of you that help. ;)

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Sat Jul 31, 2010 11:47 pm I don't think you'd spoil the movies by watching the TV series. The two only resemble each other on a superficial plot level, and the remaining movie(s) will probably not be anything like the source material.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Sat Jul 31, 2010 11:48 pm Watch the show, optionally forgetting the movies ever existed. You'll thank yourself for it later.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sat Jul 31, 2010 11:55 pm Lucretius wrote: I don't think you'd spoil the movies by watching the TV series. The two only resemble each other on a superficial plot level, and the remaining movie(s) will probably not be anything like the source material.

[]:             Quote Post by Radi » Sat Jul 31, 2010 11:57 pm That's cool. Thanks. Anyone have an order I should watch them in? I heard it's something like 1-20, 21-24, EoE, and then 25-26. Correct me if I'm wrong, I think that's just what I saw.

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Sun Aug 01, 2010 12:01 am ...

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Sun Aug 01, 2010 12:03 am Watch the director's cut versions of 21 through 24, when you get to those episodes. There's not really much reason, other than historical curiosity, to watch the original on-air cuts. And it doesn't matter a whole lot which ending you watch first, but yeah, probably go with the TV ending first, then EoE. The TV ending would seem awfully anticlimactic in comparison if you watched it last.

[]:             Quote Post by Radi » Sun Aug 01, 2010 12:06 am Seele00TextOnly wrote: just go straight from 1-26. there are Director's Cut versions of episodes 21-24, here denoted with as 21'-24'. If you can, watch 21'-24' instead of the original 21-24. They are superior in every way, nothing was edited out, only edited in. EoE is often also called 25' and 26', though you really ought to watch the original 25 and 26 before EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Sun Aug 01, 2010 12:10 am ...

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Sun Aug 01, 2010 12:11 am Definitely check out the original. And we can't stop you, but we're not going to condone illegally downloading the show. I recommend the Platinum Thinpack set; it's the cheapest you can legally get it in the US, and (oddly enough) has the highest quality transfers and encodings of the episodes. End of Eva, though, you're probably best off downloading, since the official US release of it is pretty flawed and in rather poor quality. Googling "end of eva zx" will turn up a high-quality fan-compiled release with both English and Japanese audio tracks.

[]:             Quote Post by Radi » Sun Aug 01, 2010 12:12 am Hmm. Thanks Seele and essel. That's great. I heard there's a special going on for it somewhere, and from what I've read, people like the platinum boxed-set. I'll hopefully go pick it up some-time this, or, next week, and get started. This board seems very well-rounded; I like the community. Thanks for the help. :)

[]:             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Sun Aug 01, 2010 12:15 am Well, the problem is, if you liked Rebuild, there's a decent chance you may not like episodes, say, 18 and onwards. They are not quite as cool or funny or pull of pointless fanservice as Rebuild, but are infinitely better. Good luck.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Aug 01, 2010 12:16 am Radi wrote: That's cool. Thanks. Anyone have an order I should watch them in? I heard it's something like 1-20, 21-24, EoE, and then 25-26. Correct me if I'm wrong, I think that's just what I saw.

[]:             Quote Post by Radi » Sun Aug 01, 2010 12:19 am Thanks Eva. Will do.

[]:             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Sun Aug 01, 2010 12:20 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: No. Do this: 1-20 21-24 Director's Cuts (in the Platinum boxes) 25-26 EoE

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Aug 01, 2010 12:27 am BeoX2 wrote: Does it really matter if they're all DC or not?

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Sun Aug 01, 2010 12:28 am The Director's Cuts only exist for episodes 21 through 24. The rest of the episodes only have one version, though the video quality is better on the Platinum releases. Also, don't bother with Death & Rebirth. Death is a recap of episodes 1-24, and Rebirth is the first half of EoE. Maybe consider watching Death at some point if you're really interested in it, but honestly, the only new content in it was later added into episodes 21 through 24 for the director's cuts.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Aug 01, 2010 12:29 am ^ Yay for posts saying the same thing 1 minute apart!

[]:             Quote Post by Radi » Sun Aug 01, 2010 12:36 am This it? http://www.bestbuy.com/site/Neon+Genesis+Evangelion%3A+Complete+Platinum+Collection+%5BHoliday+Limited+Edition%5D+%5B7+Discs%5D+-+DVD/9072013.p?id=1927787&skuId=9072013&st=Evangelion&lp=6&cp=1 I saw one some-where for 40. Not sure where.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Aug 01, 2010 12:37 am ^ That's one of the many Platinum Box sets. Like I said elsewhere, they're all essentially the same thing in just different packages.
